intent.problem: Agent lifecycle behavior is commonly duplicated across host-specific instructions, scripts, and mutable run state, which makes planning, independent review, execution, recovery, and final verification difficult to reproduce across agent surfaces.
intent.targetOutcome: Deliver a project-neutral, model-neutral, installable lifecycle kit with deterministic contracts, five thin semantic roles, surface adapters, synthetic conformance tests, fail-closed neutrality controls, bounded resource accounting, and release evidence.
intent.currentBehavior: The standalone repository contains only its initial legal and introductory files; no lifecycle implementation, frozen public contract, adapter, conformance suite, or release gate exists yet.
intent.targetBehavior: A host can clarify a request, freeze an independently reviewed SDD specification and implementation plan, compile a dependency DAG, execute and audit each task, remediate or refreeze safely, resume after interruption, and complete only after an independent final audit, while loading only stage-relevant context and retaining no origin-specific information.

included: A fail-closed neutrality boundary for worktrees, Git history, generated distributions, adapter bundles, and synthetic evaluation content.
A Python package and compact JSON CLI implementing specification, plan, freeze, task compilation, workflow, audit, schema, conformance, and neutrality contracts.
Five thin lifecycle skills backed by one deterministic core implementation.
Adapters for Codex, Claude, Cursor, and OpenCode using a versioned host-operation protocol and external model profiles.
Synthetic S0, S1, and S2 conformance scenarios, resource and token accounting, recovery tests, packaging, governance, and release verification.
excluded: Origin-specific profiles, paths, commands, documentation, fixtures, examples, identifiers, or copied repository history.
Bundled credentials, model names in core contracts, network services, hosted control planes, and automatic publication to external registries.
Claims that an adapter is verified without live host conformance evidence.
deferred: Additional host adapters beyond the four named v1 surfaces.
A graphical workflow console and hosted telemetry service.
Remote execution backends beyond the versioned local host protocol.

requirements.statement: The kit shall enforce a fail-closed neutrality boundary before any extraction or adapter work: it shall scan names and content across tracked, staged, untracked, ignored, reachable and unreachable Git data, recursively bounded release archives, and generated metadata; reject symlinks, submodules, LFS pointers, opaque inputs, skipped inputs, traversal, stale or in-repository deny sets; keep origin-specific deny values and detailed findings outside the repository; verify deny-set and attestation Ed25519 signatures against host-supplied external trust roots; and accept only fresh redacted attestations whose signature, key fingerprint, subject hashes, covered scopes, zero findings, zero skips, and zero opaque inputs all verify, failing closed otherwise. Conformance keys shall be generated only in temporary directories; production private keys and trust roots shall never enter the repository, logs, state, prompts, evidence payloads, or distributions.

The kit shall provide a reproducible Python 3.11 through 3.13 package foundation with one locked dependency graph, Apache-2.0 notices, stable versioning, cross-platform installation, and a single canonical implementation source.

The core shall expose versioned JSON-compatible contracts, JSON Schemas, stable error codes, normalized repository-relative paths, a provider-neutral host-operation protocol, and a pluggable change-set boundary without embedding host or model selection logic.

The core shall implement SDD tiers S0, S1, and S2 with deterministic clarification, specification validation, independent specification review, plan validation, independent plan review, immutable freeze locks, revision lineage, reopened state, and fail-closed refreeze rules.

The compiler shall transform only a frozen, hash-verified plan DAG into deterministic task packets containing task-local projections, exact write ownership, launch gates, capability requirements, validation contracts, reviewer routes, and bounded context references.

The workflow engine shall durably enforce dependency waves, approval modes, actor and reviewer independence, task attempts, per-step review, remediation, contract-change reopening, final audit, cancellation, deadlines, atomic state transitions, append-only events, crash recovery, resume, and reconciliation.

The audit service shall independently verify each task and the final implementation against the frozen specification, plan, exact write sets, change set, evidence receipts, acceptance criteria, and immutable lock, returning machine-readable findings-first verdicts without silently repairing implementation.

The host protocol shall record an end-to-end usage ledger for clarification, specification authoring and review, planning and review, implementation, task review, remediation, and final audit, preserving raw provider counters plus host-attested normalized billable tokens, prompt identities, cache usage, context bytes, tool calls, validation runs, and wall time.

The lifecycle shall apply tier- and capability-aware ceilings for context, semantic iterations, tokens, tool calls, validation runs, task attempts, evidence bytes, parallelism, and wall time; default to compact state projections; load only the active role and required references; and fail closed when a measured or attested limit is exceeded.

The lifecycle shall map clarification, specification authoring, specification review, plan authoring, plan review, implementation, task review, remediation, and final audit to provider-neutral capability profiles in external host configuration; profiles may resolve to one or different host models, but selection and ordered fallback shall be deterministic, fallback shall preserve every mandatory capability and independence class, unavailable mandatory capability shall fail closed, and every usage receipt shall record the stage, role, selected profile id, configuration digest, opaque host model identity, fallback decision, and attributed usage without embedding provider or model names in core contracts.

The package shall provide a stable compact JSON CLI for specification, plan, task compilation, workflow, audit, adapter, conformance, neutrality, and schema operations, with full state available only by explicit request and without host-specific shell wrappers in core.

The distribution shall contain exactly five canonical thin lifecycle skills for plan authoring, independent plan audit, task packet compilation, durable orchestration, and independent implementation audit; each entry shall remain stage-routed, project-neutral, surface-neutral, and no larger than 8192 bytes.

The kit shall include only synthetic, inventoried fixtures and deterministic conformance scenarios covering S0, S1, and S2 happy paths, repeated review, stale or forged artifacts, ownership conflicts, cyclic DAGs, reopen and refreeze, parallel execution, rejection and remediation, crash recovery, budget exhaustion, path attacks, adapter failure, and final verdicts across supported operating systems.

Every included surface adapter shall install and discover deterministically, validate the versioned request, response, error, result, review, cancellation, and usage envelopes, declare operation capabilities and maturity, fail closed for every unsupported operation, and pass the shared synthetic baseline. An evidence-accurate EXPERIMENTAL adapter is release-acceptable after that baseline; VERIFIED additionally requires fresh live host evidence for install, discovery, launch, wait, cancel, resume, tool execution, result collection, usage attestation, task audit, and final audit, with no missing required operation hidden by fallback.

The Codex adapter shall conform to the shared adapter baseline and map every host-supported lifecycle operation without reimplementing core semantics; absent host capabilities shall be declared unsupported and fail closed, synthetic baseline evidence is required for EXPERIMENTAL inclusion, and only fresh live evidence for the complete required operation set may promote it to VERIFIED.

The Claude adapter shall conform to the shared adapter baseline and map every host-supported lifecycle operation without reimplementing core semantics; absent host capabilities shall be declared unsupported and fail closed, synthetic baseline evidence is required for EXPERIMENTAL inclusion, and only fresh live evidence for the complete required operation set may promote it to VERIFIED.

The Cursor adapter shall conform to the shared adapter baseline and map every host-supported lifecycle operation without reimplementing core semantics; absent host capabilities shall be declared unsupported and fail closed, synthetic baseline evidence is required for EXPERIMENTAL inclusion, and only fresh live evidence for the complete required operation set may promote it to VERIFIED.

The OpenCode adapter shall conform to the shared adapter baseline and map every host-supported lifecycle operation without reimplementing core semantics; absent host capabilities shall be declared unsupported and fail closed, synthetic baseline evidence is required for EXPERIMENTAL inclusion, and only fresh live evidence for the complete required operation set may promote it to VERIFIED.

Release engineering shall build reproducible wheel, source distribution, adapter bundles, SBOM, checksums, notices, and provenance in a clean environment; run cross-platform tests and recursive neutrality scans; and block publication unless every bundled adapter's maturity claim matches its evidence.

Completion shall require an independent full implementation audit that reconciles every frozen requirement, workstream, write set, evidence receipt, resource ledger, neutrality result, release artifact, and conformance verdict and returns COMPLETE with no unresolved blocking or high-severity finding.
